name: Vaccinia_hairpin_termini
description: Vaccinia virus covalently-closed hairpin termini: ~104-nt AT-rich, incompletely-base-paired hairpin structures that seal each end of the linear dsDNA genome. Exists as two sequence isomers ('flip' and 'flop' / forms A and B) that differ by an inversion of a 60-bp tract — both isomers are present in equimolar amounts in virion DNA. During replication the hairpin is resolved at concatemeric junctions by the viral A22R holliday-junction resolvase. The hairpin is the substrate for telomere-like end maintenance and a major mapping challenge — short reads spanning the hairpin appear as palindromic 'self-mapping' artefacts; long-read mapping with explicit hairpin-aware extension is required.
expected_copy_count: 2 hairpins per linear genome (one at each terminus) in 1:1 flip:flop isomer ratio
